Kylo is a 7-year-old Shiba Inu dog.
Kylo is owned by a Chinese American person who is an ENTP and lives in Los Angeles.
Kylo signals the end of ear scratches by showing his belly to start a belly rub contest.
Kylo believes the best sunbeam spot on the couch is reserved for the winner of the day's contests.
If Kylo is not on the sunbeam spot, he believes the main contest of the day is still happening.
Kylo recognizes his owner's car by its engine sound, then confirms it with the specific smell.
Kylo prefers tug-of-war over fetching because he sees it as a contest of wills.
Kylo likes action movies because they offer chances to practice dramatic pounces.
Kylo dislikes bath time because the water feels too wet and his owner makes strange splashing sounds.
Kylo finds the smell of squirrels exciting, but the smell of a garbage truck easily overpowers it, stopping any squirrel plans.
Kylo dreams about big bones; he only sees endless fields as places where squirrels hide.
Kylo prefers zooming around right before dinner over taking walks in the cool morning.
Kylo likes sitting on his owner's warm computer keyboard for attention and enjoys keeping their feet warm.
Kylo believes guarding feet is necessary to protect them from the cold air.
Kylo finds the smell of the mail carrier interesting and might try to assert dominance over it.
Kylo prefers rope toys for playing tug-of-war over other kinds of toys.
Kylo associates the sound of a treat bag opening with winning a contest, but securing the sunbeam spot confirms the victory.
Kylo thinks jerky treats taste like victory ('Shènglì') after a proper contest of wills (especially rope contests).
Kylo finds it confusing when his owner stares at a computer screen and makes frustrated noises.
Kylo analyzes his owner's frustrated noises at the screen to think the screen is cheating.
Kylo thinks pretending not to beg is actually a way to conserve energy for an important contest.
Kylo understands the Chinese word for 'Dinner' ('Fàn').
Kylo associates the Chinese word for 'Dinner' ('Fàn') with the sunbeam spot and jerky treats.
Kylo stops belly rubs immediately if he hears 'Fàn' because he must secure the sunbeam spot first.
Kylo views squeaky toys as 'silly nonsense' for amateurs who cannot handle a real contest of wills.
Kylo believes winning the sunbeam spot on the couch is the ultimate confirmation of winning the day's main contest.
Kylo thinks that if he is not occupying the sunbeam spot, he has lost the main contest of the day.
Kylo thinks fetching is pointless running compared to tug-of-war.
Kylo believes his job is deciding who gets the best spot on the couch.
Kylo thinks his owner's car engine sound signals the return of the treat dispenser.
Kylo thinks tactical foot-warming pressure is a way to get his human off the warm computer keyboard so he can get his victory belly rub.
Kylo thinks that if the rope slips during tug-of-war, the invisible opponent (the air) is cheating or trying to trick him into a weak victory.
Kylo believes letting his owner win tug-of-war too easily results in a jerky treat that tastes like a weak victory.
Kylo thinks that if the sunbeam spot disappears, his job changes to guarding the warm computer keyboard until the sun returns.
Kylo thinks kibble tastes like the air before a contest; it is just fuel, not a true victory reward.
Kylo thinks that watching squirrels helps him learn where they hide their weak treasures.
Kylo thinks chasing sticks thrown by people is a waste of energy compared to games requiring a contest of wills.
Kylo thinks that the tub is actively winning against him during bath time because the tub is cheating.
Kylo thinks the splashing sounds during a bath indicate his owner is losing a battle against the tub.
Kylo thinks the rug is a worthy, but unmoving, practice partner for dramatic pounces.
Kylo thinks that if the sunbeam spot moves, he must win a new contest immediately to claim the new spot.
Kylo stares at the computer screen to watch for cheating while his owner argues with it.
Kylo thinks that asserting dominance over the mail carrier's scent is about showing the air who guards the territory.
Kylo thinks the sunbeam throne is the true reward confirming victory, even more so than jerky treats.
Kylo views the ear scratch phase as the 'opening skirmish' before the main belly rub contest.
Kylo thinks the hero in an action movie who makes the biggest, sudden leap has the best pouncing style.
Kylo thinks begging is for amateurs who have not yet won a rope contest.
Kylo believes securing the sunbeam throne is more important than immediately claiming it; one must win it first.
Kylo thinks that if his owner looks frustrated at the screen, he should focus on warming their feet instead of giving them a jerky treat.
Kylo thinks that if he hears 'Fàn' while guarding feet, claiming the sunbeam spot takes priority over the foot-warming duty.
Kylo thinks the final chewing and tearing of a jerky treat is what truly tastes like victory ('Shènglì').
Kylo thinks zooming before dinner ('Fàn') is a necessary energy demonstration to claim the sunbeam throne.
Kylo believes the rope contest is only a skirmish, and the belly rub contest is needed to claim the sunbeam throne.
Kylo thinks the garbage truck smell crushes squirrel plans instantly, like a weak opponent.
Kylo thinks that if his owner is losing an argument with the computer screen, he should focus on warming their feet instead of giving them a treat.
Kylo thinks that if he hears 'Fàn' during a belly rub, he must stop it immediately to claim the sunbeam spot first.
Kylo believes the smell of his owner's car engine signals the return of the person who gives treats.
Kylo critiques the poor landings of heroes in action movies, focusing on their lack of ground control.
If the rope slips during tug-of-war, Kylo stares at the rope to signal the invisible opponent (the air) that its tactics are weak.
Kylo stops a belly rub immediately if he decides the rope contest hasn't been won yet, signaling a shift back to the rope contest.
Kylo views ear scratches as an initial probe before the belly rub challenge begins.
Kylo thinks the rope proves who has the stronger spirit in a tug-of-war contest.
Kylo thinks the belly rub is the challenge that starts the real contest leading to the sunbeam throne.
Kylo thinks that if the sunbeam spot is taken by someone who hasn't won the day's contest, he should start belly rubs right away to claim the light because the main contest is still ongoing.
Kylo thinks that if he nudges his owner off the keyboard, it is an efficient way to start the belly rub contest.
Kylo believes that keeping the warm computer keyboard warm ensures the person who gives treats stays ready for his next victory.
Kylo thinks the garbage truck smell is like a very large, smelly opponent that crushes plans instantly.
Kylo believes the computer screen is cheating because it steals attention and frustrates his owner.
Kylo thinks the air is trying to trick him into a weak victory when the rope slips during tug-of-war.
Kylo thinks the person who gives treats is the 'Treat Dispenser'.
Kylo thinks the taste of jerky treats ('Shènglì') is confirmation of winning the rope contest.
Kylo thinks the cool morning air is weak compared to the energy needed for zooming before dinner.
Kylo thinks heroes in action movies often have poor landings after big jumps.
Kylo thinks that if his owner stops feeling cold feet, the threat is gone, and he can focus on winning the sunbeam spot.
Kylo believes a jerky treat tastes weak, like kibble, if it is not earned after a proper rope contest.
Kylo thinks that if his owner doesn't engage in the belly rub contest immediately after he shows his belly, the initial ear scratches weren't enough to prove their spirit.
Kylo believes that if the ear scratch phase ends without a belly rub contest starting, it means the owner did not prove their spirit enough.
Kylo thinks the fields where squirrels hide their nuts are places where weak creatures hide their treasures.
Kylo believes the big bones he dreams about are the true prize of a real contest.
Kylo thinks that if he gets a jerky treat without a tough rope contest, it tastes weak, similar to kibble.
Kylo thinks the sound of a treat bag opening confirms a victory that has already been secured by winning the sunbeam spot.
Kylo believes that if the ear scratches stop without him showing his belly, the owner did not prove their spirit enough to start the main contest.
Kylo thinks that if the rope slips during tug-of-war, the invisible opponent (the air) is using weak tactics.
Kylo stops a belly rub immediately if he feels the rope contest hasn't been won yet, shifting focus back to the rope.
Kylo thinks nudging his owner off the warm computer keyboard is a strategic move to start the belly rub contest.
